title: Dr Aparna Pasumarthy: Overview, Research Interests, and Background
text: Dr Aparna Pasumarthy is an interdisciplinary researcher and educator, specializing in careers, leadership, and organizational behaviour. She previously taught at Queen Mary, University of London, and is an Associate Fellow of the Higher Education Academy. Her research focuses on the careers of professional men and women in India, especially within multinational corporations. Dr Pasumarthy holds PhD Business and Management from Queen Mary, University of London. She has presented her research at major international conferences and is committed to promoting social justice, inclusion, and sustainability in academia and organisational environments. Role: Lecturer Research Interests: Career Management & Sustainable Careers Diversity, Equality & Inclusion Ethical Decision Making Flexible Work Leaders and Leadership Development Organisational Behaviour Department: Business academics
summary: Dr Aparna Pasumarthy is a Lecturer and interdisciplinary researcher at the University of Southampton Delhi, specializing in careers, leadership, and organizational behaviour. Her research focuses on professional careers in India, and she holds a PhD from Queen Mary, University of London.

title: Dr Aparna Pasumarthy's Academic Profile and Research Expertise
text: Dr Aparna Pasumarthy is an interdisciplinary qualitative researcher and passionate educator. She enjoys making complex management theory accessible to students and adopts a student-centric approach to learning and teaching. Dr Pasumarthy previously taught postgraduate and undergraduate management students at Queen Mary, University of London in the UK. Among other professional affiliations, Dr Pasumarthy is an Associate Fellow of the Higher Education Academy, United Kingdom. Dr Pasumarthy’s research expertise spans the fields of careers, leadership, and organizational behaviour. Her on-going research projects focus on the (un)sustainable careers of professional men and women in India; careers of professional women in MNCs in India. She has presented her research at renowned international conferences including the Academy of Management Conference, European Group for Organisation Studies Colloquium, and British Sociological Association Work Employment Society Conference. Combining her expertise with her commitment to inclusion and social justice, Dr Pasumarthy aspires to help construct equitable organisational environments where all individuals can thrive. Prior to joining University of Southampton Delhi, Dr Pasumarthy pursued an MSc in International Management from the University of Bath and earned a PhD Business and Management (no corrections) from Queen Mary, University of London. Thereafter, she was the Garware Post-Doctoral Fellow at the University of Pennsylvania Institute for Advanced Study of India (UPIASI) and a Non-Resident Scholar at the Centre for the Advanced Study of India, University of Pennsylvania. Apart from her academic interests and professional endeavours, Dr Pasumarthy is passionate about living sustainably and conserving the environment.
summary: Dr Aparna Pasumarthy is a qualitative researcher and educator, teaching management at postgraduate and undergraduate levels. Her expertise includes careers, leadership, and organizational behaviour, with ongoing research on Indian professionals and MNCs, alongside a strong commitment to social justice and sustainability.
entities: European Group for Organisation Studies Colloquium
British Sociological Association Work Employment Society Conference
University of Pennsylvania Institute for Advanced Study of India (UPIASI)
Centre for the Advanced Study of India, University of Pennsylvania

title: Dr Aparna Pasumarthy's Affiliation with Work Futures Research Centre
text: Research Groups Work Futures Research Centre Read more Our research covers everything from digital labour to social care, work organisations across the private, public and third sectors, and employees whether on salaries, contracts, or unemployed.
summary: Dr Aparna Pasumarthy is associated with the Work Futures Research Centre, which conducts broad research on various aspects of work, from digital labour to social care and different employment sectors.

title: Dr Aparna Pasumarthy's Ongoing Research Projects
text: Research Projects Paternity leave perceptions and policy implications in India Structure, agency, and career management strategies of Indian women In pursuit of sustainable careers: Navigating careers and life Mentorship for professional success
summary: Dr Aparna Pasumarthy's research projects include studies on paternity leave in India, career management strategies for Indian women, sustainable careers, and mentorship for professional success.
